evidence: The Methods section (2.6) states: "a random forest regression conducted using the R package ranger [69], and the variable importance was formally assessed at the same time using the R package vip (variable importance plot) [70]. The most highly predictive covariates from this process were then entered into the regression modelling equations." The Results and Discussion sections present Tables S5-S8 showing relative importance values but never interpret their magnitude or ranking. All substantive conclusions rest instead on regression coefficients and their p-values, E-values for causal inference robustness, and inverse probability weighting for pseudo-randomization. The data were log-transformed to address non-normality (indicating skewness), and features include mixed measurement units: percentages, mg/g concentrations, liters per capita, and USD.
